_patch_meta.description: Pre-analysis MTI classification for all 15 None-status terms in registry 007 (anxiety). G3077 classified as xref_sorrow (covered in full in grief/sorrow registries). G4328/G4329 classified extracted_thin (anticipatory/apprehension dimension present but not primary semantic range). H7599/H7600 classified extracted (antithetical pole — false security / absence of appropriate anxiety). All other core terms classified extracted.

set.status_note: Primary NT anxiety term. merimnaō — to be anxious, to have a divided, distracted mind. Core vocabulary for Matt 6:25-34 and Phil 4:6 anchor passages. 17 verses, 25 occurrences.
Primary NT anxiety noun. merimna — care, anxiety, the weight of divided concern. Appears in parable of the sower (thorns = cares of the world) and 1 Pet 5:7. 6 verses, 8 occurrences.
Primary Hebrew anxiety verb. da.ag — to be anxious, to fear, to worry. CRL to fear (061) registered. 7 verses, 7 occurrences. Full coverage.
Primary Hebrew anxiety noun. de.agah — anxiety, care, worry. CRL to sorrow (154) registered. Pro 12:25 (anxiety weighs down the heart) is anchor verse. 6 verses, 6 occurrences. Full coverage.

sar.ap.pim — disquieting thoughts, anxious inner division. Rare term (2 occ) but analytically significant: Psa 94:19 (many anxious thoughts / God's consolations) and Psa 139:23 (search me and know my thoughts). Both are inner-state introspection verses.
se.ip.pah — division of opinion, halting between two thoughts. 1 Ki 18:21 (limping between two opinions — Elijah on Carmel). Thin data (1 verse) but relevant as the divided-mind dimension of anxiety.

se.ip.pim — disquieting thoughts, anxious contemplations. Job 4:13 (thoughts from visions of the night) and Job 20:2 (haste within me). Thin (2 verses) but part of the divided-thought family alongside H5587A and H8312.

sha.an — to be at ease, to rest securely. Antithetical pole of anxiety — the absence of anxious dread. Includes both positive ease (Pro 1:33: at ease without dread of disaster, as fruit of wisdom) and morally neutral ease (Job 3:18). 5 verses, full coverage.

sha.a.nan — at ease, secure, complacent. Predominantly the condemned false-security register: Amos 6:1 (woe to those at ease in Zion), Isa 32:9/11 (complacent women rebuked), 2 Ki 19:28 (complacency as sin). Also positive: Isa 32:18 (God's people in secure dwellings). 10 verses, full coverage. Analytically important as the pathological pole — false ease that should be anxiety.

prosdokaō — to expect, look for, wait for. Primary sense is neutral expectation/attention; not an anxiety term per se. However, a subset of its 15 verse records carries genuine anticipatory-dread content: Act 27:33 (suspense on the ship), Act 28:6 (waiting for Paul to die), Mat 24:50/Luk 12:46 (vigilant suspense of judgment context). The anxiety-dimension is contextual not lexical. Analysis must work from anxiety-valenced verses only. THIN_DATA flag applies to the anxiety register specifically.

prosdokia — expectation, foreboding, apprehension. Noun form skews toward fearful expectation in classical usage (LSJ: 'more commonly fear'). Luk 21:26 (fainting with fear and foreboding of what is coming) is the clearest anxiety-register verse. Only 2 verses in export (5 occurrences); data very thin. Analysis is cautious and limited to the foreboding sense.

alupos — free from anxiety, without grief or pain. Privative adjective; the negated form illuminates the positive term. Phil 2:28: Paul sends Epaphroditus so they may be 'less anxious' (comparative). 1 verse in export (3 occurrences).

amerimnos — free from care, unconcerned. The NT positive counterpart to merimnaō. 1 Cor 7:32 (Paul's desire that believers be free from anxieties) and Mat 28:14 (guards promised they will be kept out of trouble). 2 verses, full coverage.

promerimnaō — to be anxious beforehand, to worry in advance. Intensified/prefixed form of merimnaō. Mar 13:11 (do not worry beforehand what to say when brought to trial). 1 verse, 1 occurrence. Thin but genuine anxiety vocabulary — the pre-emptive, anticipatory register.

lupē — grief, sorrow, pain of mind. Primary semantic range is grief/sorrow, not anxiety. Fully covered in grief (071) and sorrow (154) registries. CRL to sorrow (154) already registered. Excluded from Session B anxiety analysis.

record.description: G4329 prosdokia has 5 occurrences (STEP) but only 2 verse records in export. The foreboding/apprehension sense (Luk 21:26) is analytically significant but the dataset is too thin for confident conclusions. Phase 2 independent research required before synthesis-level use of this term.

G4328 prosdokaō has 20 occurrences but only 15 verses in export; anxiety-register content is present in a minority of these. The anxiety dimension is contextual not lexical. Analysis limited to anxiety-valenced verses (Act 27:33, Act 28:6, Mat 24:50/Luk 12:46 context). Full corpus review in Phase 2 recommended.

classifications.note: G3077 lupē classified xref_sorrow (covered in grief/sorrow registries). G4328/G4329 extracted_thin — anticipatory/apprehension dimension present but not primary semantic range. H7599/H7600 extracted as antithetical pole (false security / legitimate ease). Active terms for Session B analysis: 14.
